ops.text:
RESULTS E30.5 (σ_train), pending independent VERIFY. All 5 identical-recipe retrains completed (seeds 1-5, only seed varied; pyqwest 0.6.2 fix held throughout). 5 AUCs on the frozen 628: [0.9169, 0.9366, 0.9246, 0.9247, 0.9345], mean 0.9274, RANGE 0.0197. σ_train = 0.00807 (sample SD ddof=1), χ² 95% CI [0.00483, 0.02318]; the historical single-rerun estimate σ≈0.010 sits INSIDE the CI (validated). Sanity gate passed exactly before training (0.9316896744944222). auc_match=true on all 5 (analyze.py self-check; independent VERIFY still owed).

FT-vs-FT ACTIONABILITY FLOOR |Δ| ≥ 2.8016·√(SE_row² + 2σ²_train/m), SE_row=0.006008: m=1 → 0.0361; m=5 → 0.0221. THIS IS THE HEADLINE: run-to-run TRAINING noise (σ_train≈0.008) DOMINATES the paired row-sampling instrument (MDE 0.0143 @ ρ=0.9). A single-run FT-vs-FT watcher delta below ~0.036 is INDISTINGUISHABLE FROM SEED NOISE. Implications (card-v2 / Wave-1 binding): (1) Wave 1's watcher-recipe experiments (E37 MoE, E39 NS-DPO) must show |Δ|≥0.036 at m=1, OR average m seeds to lower the floor, OR be reported as within-training-noise — most recipe tweaks move AUC <0.02 and will be nulls by construction; design Wave 1 accordingly. (2) The WEEKLY PROMOTION GATE promotes a challenger on a SINGLE-run delta — if that delta is <0.036 it may be promoting training noise; the promotion criterion should incorporate σ_train (a real production finding for the served-artifact chain / 162a5dc). (3) E28's clean-label retrain being "within noise floor" is now quantitatively confirmed (σ_train swamps it). NOTE σ_train applies ONLY to comparisons of SEPARATELY-TRAINED models (recipe/seed), NOT to CAA-on-a-fixed-model (E27's base-vs-steered share the training run) — do not re-interpret E27 via σ_train.

⚠️ REPORT BUG (flag, VERIFY to confirm): results.json total_spend_usd=135.28 is WRONG — it summed the per-run CUMULATIVE train_cost_usd (9.01+18.05+27.09+36.10+45.03=135.28) instead of taking the final cumulative. ACTUAL Tinker training spend = $45.03 (seed-5 cumulative), UNDER the $56 SpendGuard cap (which did not abort → confirms actual ≤ $56). Real spend is fine; only the report field is buggy. Artifacts: ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E30/e30_5/{out/results.json, runs/seed{1..5}/, run_all.log}.
